item.title: Game Development Series Part 4: 2D Game Development
item.description: A sprite is a 2D image used in games. Every character, enemy, item, and background element you see in 2D games is a sprite. Sprite sheets combine multiple sprites into a single image file for efficiency. Single Sprites vs Sprite Sheet: Single Files (Slow): Sprite Sheet (Fast): ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 5: 3D Game Development
item.description: Moving from 2D to 3D adds a third dimension: depth (Z-axis) . Every object now has position, rotation, and scale in three dimensions, making spatial reasoning more complex but enabling immersive worlds. 3D Coordinate System (Left-Handed - Unity): Y (Up) │ │ Z (Forward) │ ╱ │ ╱ │ ╱ │╱ ─────────────●─
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 10: Game AI Systems
item.description: Pathfinding is how AI figures out how to get from point A to point B. It's essential for enemies chasing players, NPCs navigating towns, and units in strategy games. Grid as a Graph: ┌───┬───┬───┬───┬───┐ │ S │ │ │ █ │ │ S = Start ├───┼───┼───┼───┼───┤ G = Goal │ │ █ │ │ █ │ │ █ = Obstacle ├───┼───┼
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 7: Audio & Sound Design
item.description: Audio is often called the "invisible 50%" of game experience. Great audio makes players feel emotions, understand game states, and become immersed. Poor audio breaks immersion instantly. The Emotional Impact of Audio: Game Moment Without Audio With Audio ─────────────────────────────────────────────
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 2: Choosing a Game Engine
item.description: A game engine is a software framework that provides the foundational systems needed to create games. Instead of building everything from scratch—rendering, physics, audio, input handling—you get pre-built, tested components that work together. Unity is the world's most popular game engine, powering
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 9: Game Design Fundamentals
item.description: Understanding player psychology is the foundation of good game design. Games are experiences crafted to evoke specific emotions, satisfy needs, and create memorable moments. Bartle's Player Types (MMO-focused): Achievers Explorers ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ │ 🏆 "What can │ │ 🗺️ "What'
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 1: Introduction to Game Development
item.description: Game development is the art and science of creating interactive experiences that captivate players. Unlike watching a movie or reading a book, games respond to player input in real-time, creating dynamic experiences that are different every time. Think of game development as building an interactive
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 11: Multiplayer & Networking
item.description: Choosing the right network architecture is one of the most important decisions in multiplayer game development. It affects gameplay feel, security, and scalability. graph TD subgraph CS["Client-Server Model"] SRV["Authoritative Server"] CL1["Client 1"] --> SRV CL2["Client 2"] --> SRV CL3["Client 3"]
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 6: Physics & Collision Detection
item.description: A Rigidbody is a component that enables physics simulation on a game object. Once added, the physics engine takes over—the object responds to gravity, forces, collisions, and momentum. Physics Engine Components: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ PHYSICS ENGINE │ │ │ │ ┌──
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 13: Building Your Portfolio
item.description: Your portfolio showcases your skills to potential employers. Quality matters more than quantity— 3-5 polished games beat 20 unfinished prototypes. Portfolio Project Strategy: Skill Level Project Type Purpose ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── BEGINNER Classic Game Clone Sh
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 12: Professional Workflow
item.description: Version control is essential for game development—it tracks changes, enables collaboration, and provides safety nets when things go wrong. Git is the industry standard. Git Workflow: Working Directory Staging Area Repository │ │ │ │ git add │ git commit │ │────────────────────→│─────────────────────
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 3: Programming Basics for Games
item.description: The game loop is the heartbeat of every video game. Unlike traditional programs that wait for user input, games must constantly update and render, creating the illusion of real-time interaction. Understanding the game loop is the most fundamental concept in game programming. // The simplest game loo
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)

item.title: Game Development Series Part 8: Building & Publishing Games
item.description: A build is a compiled, playable version of your game that runs on a target platform without the development environment. Creating builds involves configuration, compilation, and packaging. Build Types: Development Build: Release Build: ┌─────────────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────────┐ │ ✓ Debu
item.author: wasil.zafar@gmail.com (Wasil Zafar)
